title: Basic Risotto Ingredient Calculator
explanation: Adjust ingredient amounts for any number of servings to ensure proper proportions and cooking time.

cells:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

# Slider inputs (global variables)
shallot_amt = shallot_count
 garlic_amt = garlic_cloves
 stock_ml = stock_cups * 240

print(f"Shallots: {shallot_amt}")
print(f"Garlic cloves: {garlic_amt}")
print(f"Stock (ml): {stock_ml}")

plt.bar(['Stock'], [stock_ml])
plt.title('Chicken Stock Amount')
plt.ylabel('Milliliters')
plt.show()

title: Risotto Recipe Scaler
explanation: Scaling the ingredient amounts lets viewers adapt the risotto recipe to any desired number of servings.

title: Risotto ingredient scaling
explanation: Adjusting the quantities of rice, wine, saffron, and stock lets you prepare the correct portion size while preserving flavor and timing.

cells:
import math

# Compute ingredient amounts
rice_cups = servings * 0.25   # 1/4 cup per serving
wine_cups = wine_cups          # from slider
saffron_pinsches = saffron_pinsches
stock_per_ladle = stock_ladle_cups
total_stock_cups = stock_additions * stock_per_ladle

# Total cooking time (minutes)
toast_minutes = 0.5   # 30 seconds = 0.5 min
cook_minutes = servings * 5  # approx 5 min per serving
total_time_minutes = toast_minutes + cook_minutes

print(f"Rice: {rice_cups:.2f} cups")
print(f"White wine: {wine_cups:.2f} cups")
print(f"Saffron: {saffron_pinsches} pinch(es)")
print(f"Stock per ladle: {stock_per_ladle:.2f} cup(s), total stock: {total_stock_cups:.2f} cups")
print(f"Total time (min): {total_time_minutes:.1f}")

title: Risotto Recipe Scaler
explanation: Adjusting ingredient quantities lets you scale the risotto recipe to any number of servings.

params.expr: total_rice = rice_per_serving * servings; total_stock = stock_per_serving * servings
